#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

podman build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .
podman run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh

file.content:
# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

Two submodules:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
for the full list.

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `hybrid_vsim/build/vsim`
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Orchestrates the hybrid simulator build: QEMU 9.x + vsim + TCG plugin.
3	# Run subcommands individually, or run with no args to build everything.
4	
5	set -euo pipefail
6	
7	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
8	QEMU_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
9	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
10	PLUGIN_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/qemu_plugin"
11	QEMU_BUILD="$QEMU_DIR/build"
12	VSIM_BUILD="$VSIM_DIR/build"
13	
14	JOBS="$(nproc)"
15	
16	c_step()  { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
17	c_ok()    { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
18	c_err()   { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
19	
20	require_dir() {
21	  if [[ ! -d "$1" ]]; then
22	    c_err "missing $1 - did you run \`git submodule update --init --recursive\`?"
23	    exit 1
24	  fi
25	}
26	
27	build_qemu() {
28	  require_dir "$QEMU_DIR"
29	  c_step "QEMU: configure (if needed)"
30	  if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
31	    mkdir -p "$QEMU_BUILD"
32	    (cd "$QEMU_BUILD" && ../configure \
33	      --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
34	      --enable-plugins \
35	      --disable-werror)
36	  fi
37	  c_step "QEMU: build qemu-system-riscv64"
38	  if [[ -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" ]]; then
39	    ninja -C "$QEMU_BUILD" qemu-system-riscv64
40	  else
41	    make -C "$QEMU_BUILD" -j"$JOBS" qemu-system-riscv64
42	  fi
43	  c_ok "QEMU built: $QEMU_BUILD/qemu-system-riscv64"
44	}
45	
46	build_vsim() {
47	  require_dir "$VSIM_DIR"
48	  c_step "vsim: configure (if needed)"
49	  if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_BUILD/CMakeCache.txt" ]]; then
50	    cmake -S "$VSIM_DIR" -B "$VSIM_BUILD" -G Ninja \
51	      -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON \
52	      -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
53	  fi
54	  c_step "vsim: build"
55	  cmake --build "$VSIM_BUILD" -j"$JOBS"
56	  c_ok "vsim built: $VSIM_BUILD/vsim"
57	}
58	
59	build_plugin() {
60	  require_dir "$PLUGIN_DIR"
61	  c_step "plugin: build libhybrid_handoff.so"
62	  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
63	  c_ok "plugin built: $PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
64	}
65	
66	run_tests() {
67	  require_dir "$VSIM_BUILD"
68	  c_step "ctest: hybrid-e2e label"
69	  (cd "$VSIM_BUILD" && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure)
70	}
71	
72	clean_all() {
73	  c_step "clean: remove build artifacts"
74	  rm -rf "$QEMU_BUILD" "$VSIM_BUILD"
75	  rm -f  "$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
76	  c_ok "cleaned"
77	}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Orchestrates the hybrid simulator build: QEMU 9.x + vsim + TCG plugin.
# Run subcommands individually, or run with no args to build everything.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
PLUGIN_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/qemu_plugin"
QEMU_BUILD="$QEMU_DIR/build"
VSIM_BUILD="$VSIM_DIR/build"

JOBS="$(nproc)"

c_step()  { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
c_ok()    { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
c_err()   { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require_dir() {
  if [[ ! -d "$1" ]]; then
    c_err "missing $1 - did you run \`git submodule update --init --recursive\`?"
    exit 1
  fi
}

build_qemu() {
  require_dir "$QEMU_DIR"
  c_step "QEMU: configure (if needed)"
  if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$QEMU_BUILD"
    (cd "$QEMU_BUILD" && ../configure \
      --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
      --enable-plugins \
      --disable-werror)
  fi
  c_step "QEMU: build qemu-system-riscv64"
  if [[ -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" ]]; then
    ninja -C "$QEMU_BUILD" qemu-system-riscv64
  else
    make -C "$QEMU_BUILD" -j"$JOBS" qemu-system-riscv64
  fi
  c_ok "QEMU built: $QEMU_BUILD/qemu-system-riscv64"
}

build_vsim() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_DIR"
  c_step "vsim: configure (if needed)"
  if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_BUILD/CMakeCache.txt" ]]; then
    cmake -S "$VSIM_DIR" -B "$VSIM_BUILD" -G Ninja \
      -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON \
      -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  fi
  c_step "vsim: build"
  cmake --build "$VSIM_BUILD" -j"$JOBS"
  c_ok "vsim built: $VSIM_BUILD/vsim"
}

build_plugin() {
  require_dir "$PLUGIN_DIR"
  c_step "plugin: build libhybrid_handoff.so"
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  c_ok "plugin built: $PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
}

run_tests() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_BUILD"
  c_step "ctest: hybrid-e2e label"
  (cd "$VSIM_BUILD" && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure)
}

clean_all() {
  c_step "clean: remove build artifacts"
  rm -rf "$QEMU_BUILD" "$VSIM_BUILD"
  rm -f  "$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  c_ok "cleaned"
}

usage() {
  cat <<EOF
usage: $(basename "$0") [qemu|vsim|plugin|test|clean|all]

  (no args)  same as 'all'
  qemu       configure + build hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  vsim       configure + build hybrid_vsim/build/vsim (VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON)
  plugin     build hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
  test       ctest -L hybrid-e2e (in vsim build dir)
  clean      remove both build dirs and the plugin .so
  all        qemu, then vsim, then plugin (in this order)
EOF
}

cmd="${1:-all}"
case "$cmd" in
  qemu)   build_qemu ;;
  vsim)   build_vsim ;;
  plugin) build_plugin ;;
  test)   run_tests ;;
  clean)  clean_all ;;
  all)    build_qemu; build_vsim; build_plugin ;;
  -h|--help|help) usage ;;
  *)      c_err "unknown command: $cmd"; usage; exit 2 ;;
esac

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

## How to run

From repo root:
```
./build.sh           # builds qemu + vsim + plugin
./build.sh test      # cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
```

The suite is registered in `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake:99-150`, driven by shell scripts under `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/` (`roundtrip_e2e.sh`, `rt_shared_mem.sh`, `rt_corrupt_state.sh`, `rt_no_qemu_binary.sh`, `rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh`). Each test returns 77 → CTest skip when binaries/ELFs are missing.

Caveat - `roundtrip_e2e.sh:29` defaults `VSIM_QEMU` to a hardcoded `/local/nick/qemu_v5/...` path. `build.sh:69` runs ctest without exporting `VSIM_QEMU`, so on this workspace the e2e cases will skip unless you do:
```
VSIM_QEMU=$PWD/hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64 \
VSIM_PLUGIN=$PWD/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so \
ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
```
(or fix `run_tests` in `build.sh` to export those.)

## Move to hybrid_sim?

Recommend **no** for the scripts and ctest registration, **yes** for orchestration.

- Keep in `hybrid_vsim`: scripts live next to the ELF fixtures (`hybrid/test/*.elf`), the vsim CMake already owns the ctest graph with proper SKIP semantics, and a standalone clone of `hybrid_vsim` should still be testable.
- Add in `hybrid_sim`: have `build.sh test` export `VSIM_QEMU` / `VSIM_PLUGIN` / `VSIM_BIN` pointing at the workspace-built artifacts, so the harness picks them up instead of the hardcoded `/local/nick` default. Cheap fix, keeps tests co-located with the SUT.

Want me to patch `build.sh:66-70` to export those env vars?
Which part - the run instructions, or the move-vs-keep recommendation?

**Keep test scripts in `hybrid_vsim`. Add only orchestration glue in `hybrid_sim`.**

Reasons to keep them in `hybrid_vsim`:
1. The scripts depend on artifacts that live in `hybrid_vsim`: ELF fixtures under `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/`, the vsim binary, and the plugin source. Moving the scripts splits them from the things they test.
2. `hybrid_vsim/CMakeLists.txt` already registers them as ctest cases. `ctest -L hybrid-e2e` works out of the vsim build dir.
3. If someone clones `hybrid_vsim` standalone (not via this workspace), they can still run the e2e suite.

What `hybrid_sim` should own:
- A `build.sh test` that exports `VSIM_QEMU=$ROOT/hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (and `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`) before invoking ctest, so the scripts find the workspace-built QEMU instead of the hardcoded `/local/nick/qemu_v5/...` default in `roundtrip_e2e.sh:29`.

That is the only thing missing today. The tests themselves do not need to move.

Then move them. I was wrong.

Under that framing:
- `hybrid_qemu` = QEMU fork + plugin source. Self-contained.
- `hybrid_vsim` = vsim sources + vsim-internal unit tests.
- `hybrid_sim` = orchestration: build, run, **and the cross-component contract tests**.

What belongs in `hybrid_sim/tests/`:
- `roundtrip_e2e.sh` and the `rt_*.sh` drivers - they spawn QEMU + vsim + plugin together; they are the orchestration's contract.
- The `add_test(... LABELS hybrid-e2e)` block currently in `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake:99-150`, lifted into a top-level `hybrid_sim/CMakeLists.txt` (or a thin Makefile wrapping ctest).
- The ELF fixtures under `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/` - move with them, since they only exist to feed these scripts.

What stays in `hybrid_vsim`:
- Unit tests that don't spawn QEMU: `state_abi.test.cpp`, `shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp`, `shared_mem_offset.test.cpp`, `state_loader.test.cpp`, `abstract_command.test.cpp`, `csr_trigger.test.cpp`, `handoff_controller.test.cpp`, `insn_match.test.cpp`, `memory_map.test.cpp`, `resume_driver.test.cpp`, `resume_from_qemu.test.cpp`, `state_drain.test.cpp`, `gdbstub_client.test.cpp`. These exercise vsim internals only.

This also kills the hardcoded `/local/nick/qemu_v5` default - paths become workspace-relative because the test now lives in the workspace.

`hybrid_qemu` is unaffected.

From a fresh clone:
```

./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e from hybrid_sim build dir
```

`hybrid_vsim/build/`, with no skips caused by the workspace layout (no
hardcoded `/local/nick/...`).

## Ground rules

- One feature per session. Tidy First: every feature here is **structural**
  (no behavior change). Each commit message starts with `structural:`.

  same pass/skip set until the registration is migrated (F4).

  roadmap is complete.

hardcoded `/local/nick/qemu_v5/...` from the e2e scripts. All artifact
paths come in via env: `VSIM_BIN`, `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_ELF`.
Scripts exit 77 (CTest skip) if any required env var is unset or its
target is missing. Update `HybridConfig.cmake` to pass those env vars
explicitly via `set_tests_properties(... ENVIRONMENT ...)` or in the
`COMMAND` line, sourced from CMake variables that already know the build
layout.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh

if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
else
  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
fi

# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
# under nounset).
_hybrid_setup_main() {
  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
    emulate -L sh
    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
  else
    local -
    set -euo pipefail
  fi

  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR
  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"

  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

  step "1/2 building qemu"
  "$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

  step "2/2 building vsim"
  "$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
  fi
}

_hybrid_setup_main
unset -f _hybrid_setup_main step ok err 2>/dev/null
unset _SETUP_SRC

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build hybrid_qemu out-of-tree under build/qemu/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
BUILD="$ROOT/build/qemu"

if [[ ! -x "$SRC/configure" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/configure - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$BUILD"

if [[ ! -f "$BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
  (cd "$BUILD" && "$SRC/configure" \
    --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
    --disable-werror \
    --disable-libnfs \
    --disable-werror)
fi

make -C "$BUILD" CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64

```sh

# build the plugin
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin \
  QEMU_ROOT="$PWD/hybrid_qemu"

# point the e2e scripts at workspace artifacts and run

export VSIM_PLUGIN="$PWD/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so"
ctest --test-dir hybrid_vsim/build -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
```

ctest --test-dir hybrid_vsim/build -R test_e2e_handoff_roundtrip -V

1. `build_qemu.sh:17` does not pass `--enable-plugins`. Recent QEMU enables plugins by default on most targets, but if `-plugin` errors at runtime, add `--enable-plugins`.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh

if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
else
  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
fi

# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
# under nounset).
_hybrid_setup_main() {
  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
    emulate -L sh
    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
  else
    local -
    set -euo pipefail
  fi

  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR
  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"

  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

  step "1/2 building qemu"
  "$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

  step "2/2 building vsim"
  "$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
  fi
}

_hybrid_setup_main
unset -f _hybrid_setup_main step ok err 2>/dev/null
unset _SETUP_SRC

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build hybrid_qemu out-of-tree under build/qemu/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
BUILD="$ROOT/build/qemu"

if [[ ! -x "$SRC/configure" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/configure - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$BUILD"

if [[ ! -f "$BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
  (cd "$BUILD" && "$SRC/configure" \
    --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
    --disable-werror \
    --disable-libnfs \
    --disable-werror)
fi

make -C "$BUILD" CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64

content.text: Added. Note this only affects fresh configures - if `build/qemu/build.ninja` already exists, the configure block is skipped. To pick up the flag on an existing tree, `rm -rf build/qemu` first.
